_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot)
_meta.note: Charlotte NC City Council batch (5): Ajmera, Driggs, Mitchell, Mazuera Arias, Mayo. Evidence-based local-tier finalization. Roster web-verified June 2026 (all five won the Nov 4 2025 city election; Mazuera Arias and Mayo are freshmen sworn in Dec 1 2025). Scores reset to null where no evidence; FALSE only where a documented vote/statement/endorsement exists; TRUE only for documented alignment.

profile.background: CPA; on Charlotte City Council since Dec 2017 (appointed District 5, then elected at-large); Budget Committee chair; born in Surat, India, immigrated ~2003; lost 2020 Dem primary for NC State Treasurer; re-elected Nov 2025 as top at-large vote-getter.

dimple-ajmera.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified current: won re-election Nov 4 2025 as top at-large vote-getter (Ballotpedia; WBTV 11/5/25). HARD evidence: voted with the 8-3 majority for the June 2019 noise-ordinance 'buffer zones' restricting amplified pro-life protest within 150 ft of medical facilities, framing abortion as 'access to healthcare' (QC Nerve 6/25/19), and joined a 2024 Biden-campaign reproductive-care roundtable (sanctity_of_life FALSE x2). Voted for the unanimous Aug 2021 SOGI nondiscrimination ordinance — 'We have to lead by example for future generations' (WBTV/WCNC 8/9/21; biblical_marriage q2+q4 FALSE). Voted for the June 2026 FY2027 budget with a 1.89-cent property-tax increase despite having argued for flat rates in Jan 2025 (WBTV 6/9/26, QCity Metro 1/28/25; economic_stewardship FALSE, mixed record noted). Publicly championed the budget's 10% police/fire raises — 'Public safety is a team sport' — affirmative advocacy, so public_justice q0 TRUE; note she pushed an external CMPD audit in 2020 with budget-reallocation framing (audit ≠ defunding). Forcefully opposed the Nov 2025 federal Border Patrol/ICE operation — agents 'terrorizing the community,' families 'racially profiled, targeted... harassed' (WFAE 11/12/25, NPR 11/19/25; border q1 FALSE; no explicit sanctuary-policy stance found, q2 null). Endorsed by Everytown for Gun Safety — single-issue gun-control endorsement (campaign endorsements page; self_defense q1 FALSE). Religion not publicly stated. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on school choice/parental rights, public prayer, or election administration — null, not penalized.

0.note: Frames abortion as 'access to healthcare' (2019 buffer-zone vote remarks); joined 2024 reproductive-care advocacy roundtable — documented contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Spoke for and voted with the 8-3 majority for 150-ft amplified-sound buffer zones curbing pro-life protest at clinics (June 2019) — active opposition, not abolition advocacy.

2.note: Voted for the Aug 2021 ordinance adding gender-identity/gender-expression protections: 'We have to lead by example for future generations.'

4.note: Voted for the unanimous 2021 SOGI nondiscrimination ordinance embedding LGBTQ identity in city policy.

2.note: Voted for the June 2026 FY2027 budget with a 1.89-cent property-tax increase. Mixed record: as Budget chair she argued against a tax increase in Jan 2025, but the final vote was yes; no budget NO vote found.

0.note: Publicly championed the FY2027 budget's 10% police/fire raises — 'Public safety is a team sport' (June 2026) — affirmative advocacy beyond a routine vote. Noted: pushed an external CMPD audit in 2020 with reallocation framing; an audit is oversight, not defunding.

1.note: Condemned the Nov 2025 federal deportation operation: agents 'terrorizing the community,' families 'racially profiled, targeted, questioned, detained and harassed' — documented opposition to deportation enforcement.

2.note: Opposed the federal operation but no explicit sanctuary-policy position found (NC law preempts sanctuary cities).

1.note: Endorsed by Everytown for Gun Safety (listed on her own endorsements page) — single-issue gun-control endorsement.

profile.background: Retired Goldman Sachs finance executive; Princeton economics BA; former U.S. Army captain; on council since Nov 2013 (Ballantyne/south Charlotte); past Budget and Governance chair; the council's lone Republican after Nov 2025.

ed-driggs.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified current: re-elected unopposed Nov 4 2025; now the council's only Republican (Axios Charlotte 11/5/25; Ballotpedia). HARD evidence: voted NO (8-3 loss) on the June 2019 noise-ordinance buffer zones aimed at pro-life sidewalk counselors outside the Latrobe Dr. clinic — 'the ordinance as proposed is aimed at the Latrobe protesters, and it was pretending to be something else' — defending the prayer/witness groups' public presence (WFAE 6/25/19, AFA 5/24/19; christian_liberty q0 TRUE; no direct abortion-position statement found, sanctity_of_life null). LGBTQ record split: voted NO on the Feb 2016 trans-bathroom ordinance calling it 'totally geared towards the needs of one group,' but YES on the unanimous Aug 2021 SOGI nondiscrimination ordinance (WFAE 8/9/21) — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE on the controlling 2021 vote, q2 null (mixed). Fiscal restraint is his strongest area: voted NO on the June 2026 FY2027 budget over its 1.89-cent property-tax increase ('I have an obligation to them not to support this budget'), NO on the prior tax-raising budget (WSOC/WBTV 6/9/26; economic_stewardship TRUE). Voted NO on the June 2020 amendment defunding CMPD chemical agents, calling it a 'gratuitous dig at police,' and criticized SAFE Charlotte's results in Jan 2025 (WSOC 6/2020, WFAE 1/13/25; public_justice q0 TRUE). On immigration: 'hard line on identifying and deporting illegal immigrants who commit crimes, but I'm not sure this is the way to do it' (CNN 11/17/25) — supports criminal-alien deportation only, short of the q1 bar, null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on elections, guns, schools, or personal faith — null, not penalized.

2.note: Mixed: NO on the 2016 trans-bathroom ordinance ('totally geared towards the needs of one group') but YES on the 2021 gender-identity NDO — no clean alignment either way.

4.note: Voted YES on the unanimous Aug 2021 SOGI nondiscrimination ordinance (while raising small-business concerns). Earlier voted NO on the 2016 LGBT ordinance — the controlling recent vote is the 2021 yes.

0.note: Voted NO on the 2019 buffer-zone ordinance targeting pro-life sidewalk prayer counselors, publicly calling it disguised viewpoint targeting; also flagged 'Christian business owners' concerns in the 2021 NDO debate.

2.note: Voted NO on the FY2027 budget (9-2) over its 1.89-cent property-tax increase — 'I have an obligation to them not to support this budget'; also voted against the prior tax-raising budget. Long-time Budget Committee leadership, fiscal-restraint record.

0.note: Voted NO on the June 2020 CMPD chemical-agent defund amendment, calling it a 'gratuitous dig at police'; publicly criticized SAFE Charlotte's lack of results (Jan 2025) — affirmative pro-police record.

1.note: 'Hard line on identifying and deporting illegal immigrants who commit crimes, but I'm not sure this is the way to do it' (Nov 2025) — supports criminal-alien deportation, short of the mandatory-deportation-of-all bar; not contrary either.

set.office: City Council, At-Large / Mayor Pro Tem

profile.religion: Presbyterian (member, C.N. Jenkins Memorial Presbyterian Church)
profile.background: Third-generation Charlottean; B.A. North Carolina Central University; District 2 1999-2013, at-large 2015-Jan 2021 (resigned for R.J. Leeper CEO role), returned Sept 2022; former National League of Cities president; elected Mayor Pro Tem Dec 1 2025.

james-mitchell-jr.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified current: re-elected at-large Nov 4 2025; chosen Mayor Pro Tem Dec 1 2025 (WFAE 12/2/25). The 2022-23 R.J. Leeper ownership-disclosure probe ended with the Mecklenburg DA finding 'no basis' to prosecute (WCCB 1/5/23). HARD evidence: voted YES on the FY2025 budget (8-3, June 2024) including a 1.5-cent property-tax increase — the NO votes were Driggs, Bokhari, Johnson (WCNC 6/2024; economic_stewardship FALSE; he praised the no-increase FY2026 budget the next year, noted). Called the Nov 2025 federal Border Patrol/ICE operation 'the worst period that we ever experienced in Charlotte, North Carolina' and lamented lacking authority to stop it (WCCB 11/18/25; border q1 FALSE). public_justice q0 left null: he voted against the 2020 external CMPD audit and never joined defund rhetoric, but no affirmative public pro-police advocacy found — routine budget support doesn't meet the bar. He was OFF council (Leeper stint) for the Aug 2021 NDO vote, so no LGBTQ voting record — null. Publicly identifies as a Presbyterian church member (campaign bio). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, marriage/trans policy, schools, elections, guns — null, not penalized.

2.note: Voted YES on the FY2025 budget with a 1.5-cent property-tax increase (8-3; the dissenters were Driggs, Bokhari, Johnson). Did praise the FY2026 no-tax-increase budget; no budget NO vote found.

0.note: Never joined defund rhetoric and voted against the 2020 external CMPD audit, but no affirmative public pro-police advocacy found — routine budget support doesn't meet the bar.

1.note: Called the Nov 2025 federal deportation operation (130+ arrests) 'the worst period that we ever experienced in Charlotte, North Carolina' — documented opposition to deportation enforcement.

profile.background: Age 27; born in Colombia, formerly undocumented, naturalized 2021; Queens University grad, NYU MPA; ex-staffer for Rep. Ocasio-Cortez; openly queer; Charlotte's first Gen Z, first Latino, first formerly undocumented council member; unseated incumbent Marjorie Molina in the Sept 2025 primary by 34 votes; sworn in Dec 1 2025.

jd-mazuera-arias.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified current: first-term District 5 member, won the Nov 4 2025 general after a recount-confirmed 34-vote primary win over incumbent Molina; sworn in Dec 1 2025 (WFAE 9/9/25, WBTV 11/5/25, charlottenc.gov). HARD evidence: jointly endorsed by the LGBTQ+ Victory Fund and the FFRF Action Fund in 2025 — the FFRF endorsement requires 'a clear commitment to keeping religion out of government' (ffrfaction.org 11/21/25; christian_liberty q3 FALSE, biblical_marriage q0/q4 FALSE per the single-issue-endorsement rule). Openly queer; 'We're going to take transgender rights here' (Charlotte Post 1/1/26) and 'our transgender brothers and sisters fighting for the right to exist' (WFAE 6/4/26; biblical_marriage q2 FALSE). Voted YES on the June 2026 FY2027 budget with a 1.89-cent property-tax increase (WFAE 6/8/26 — only Driggs and Mayfield voted no; economic_stewardship FALSE). Immigration is his strongest documented area: on the Nov 2025 CBP operation — 'This is not about public safety... It is about fear. It's about quotas. And it's about control' (QC News 11/2025); platform: immigrant legal-defense fund, bar CMPD from immigration-status inquiries, 'people's dignity and safety shouldn't depend on their papers' (Enlace Latino NC 8/25/25; border q1+q2 FALSE). Abortion: only a passing supportive reference to reproductive rights (WFAE 6/4/26) — too thin to score, null. public_justice q0 null: prevention-focused platform ('invest in detectives, prevention'), no defund language, no affirmative pro-police advocacy. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on guns, elections, school choice — null, not penalized.

0.note: Only a passing supportive reference to 'a woman fighting for reproductive rights' — too thin to score either way.
LGBTQ+ Victory Fund endorsement (single-issue endorsement = hard evidence); openly queer LGBTQ-equality advocate — documented contrary to the one-man-one-woman definition.

2.note: 'We're going to take transgender rights here' (1/2026); 'our transgender brothers and sisters fighting for the right to exist' (6/2026) — direct advocacy contrary to rejecting gender ideology.

4.note: Victory Fund-endorsed openly queer member publicly advancing LGBTQ rights in city policy.

3.note: Accepted the FFRF Action Fund's express endorsement, which requires 'a clear commitment to keeping religion out of government' — single-issue endorsement contrary to public-square prayer/symbols.

2.note: Voted YES on the FY2027 budget (9-2, June 2026) with a 1.89-cent property-tax increase (only Driggs and Mayfield voted no).

0.note: Prevention-first platform ('invest in detectives, prevention, and safe neighborhoods'); no defund language but no affirmative pro-police advocacy — doesn't meet the bar either way.

1.note: On the Nov 2025 CBP deportation operation: 'This is not about public safety. It is not about finding criminals. It is about fear. It's about quotas. And it's about control.' Signed the Democratic officials' joint statement against it.

2.note: Platform: bar CMPD from asking immigration status in routine stops, city immigrant legal-defense fund, 'people's dignity and safety shouldn't depend on their papers' — sanctuary-style limits on enforcement cooperation.

profile.background: Former CMS middle-school math teacher (2012-2020); TreesCharlotte community engagement manager; Kings Creek HOA president and founder of Transforming Nations Ford (southwest Charlotte); unseated indicted incumbent Tiawana Brown in the Sept 2025 primary; sworn in Dec 1 2025.

joi-mayo.notes_set: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE local refinement] Roster verified current: first-term District 3 member — defeated indicted incumbent Tiawana Brown 49.5%-25% in the Sept 2025 Dem primary, won the Nov 4 2025 general with ~73%, sworn in Dec 1 2025 (WFAE 9/9/25, Ballotpedia, charlottenc.gov). Tenure too short for a meaningful budget-vote record (took office after FY2026 adoption; FY2027 role not individually documented) — economic null. The one hard scoreable position: during the Nov 2025 federal 'Operation Charlotte's Web' raids she emailed constituents 'Charlotte is a welcoming city... Sudden, heavy-handed actions do not make us safer; they disrupt families, harm local businesses, and erode trust' (Queen City Nerve 11/2025; border q1 FALSE; no explicit sanctuary-policy stance, q2 null). public_justice q0 null: prevention-first platform (violence interrupters, SAFE Charlotte-style), collaborated with CMPD as a neighborhood leader, no defund rhetoric — doesn't meet the affirmative-advocacy bar either way. Endorsed by the Black Political Caucus of Charlotte-Mecklenburg (general, not single-issue). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, LGBTQ/marriage, schools policy, prayer/religion, elections, guns, or state preemption — null, not penalized. Record is honestly thin: one documented contrary position, everything else null.

2.note: Took office Dec 2025 — no full budget-cycle vote record yet; gave only conditional support for the 2025 transit sales-tax referendum (anti-displacement conditions). Too thin to score.

0.note: Prevention-first platform (violence interrupters); collaborated with CMPD as a neighborhood leader; no defund rhetoric — but no affirmative pro-police advocacy. Doesn't meet the bar either way.

1.note: On the Nov 2025 federal raids: 'Charlotte is a welcoming city... Sudden, heavy-handed actions do not make us safer; they disrupt families, harm local businesses, and erode trust' — documented opposition to the deportation operation.

2.note: 'Welcoming city' language but no explicit sanctuary-policy position (NC law preempts sanctuary cities).
